title: Beat Bad Breath with These 10 Superb Herbal Teas
intro_desc: Herbal teas for bad breath are a natural remedy that helps to combat halitosis, a condition characterized by persistent and unpleasant breath odor. These teas are made from various herbs that possess antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce the bacteria and germs that cause bad breath. The best herbal teas for bad breath include Eucalyptus globulus, Mentha x piperita, Zingiber officinale, Cinnamomum verum, Cymbopogon citratus, Thymus vulgaris, Salvia officinalis, Cinnamomum zeylanicum, Glycyrrhiza glabra, and Lavandula angustifolia. If left untreated, bad breath can lead to social isolation, decreased self-esteem, and strained relationships, ultimately affecting one's quality of life and overall well-being.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita tea, also known as peppermint tea, is a popular remedy for bad breath. The main reason for this is its antibacterial properties, which help kill the bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath. Peppermint tea contains a compound called menthol, which has a cooling effect and helps freshen the breath. When you drink peppermint tea, the menthol helps to kill the bacteria and reduce inflammation in the mouth. This makes it harder for bacteria to grow and multiply, resulting in fresher breath. Additionally, peppermint tea has a calming effect on the stomach, which can help to reduce nausea and vomiting, both of which can contribute to bad breath. By reducing nausea and killing bacteria, peppermint tea helps to keep the mouth and breath clean and fresh.

remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal teas for bad breath
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Rosmarinus officinalis herbal tea that helps with bad breath, start by gathering fresh or dried rosemary leaves. If using fresh rosemary, simply chop the leaves into smaller pieces and set them aside. If using dried rosemary, you can skip this step. Next, boil a pot of water and add one tablespoon of rosemary leaves for every eight ounces of water. Let the leaves steep in the boiling water for about 5-7 minutes, allowing the herbs to release their flavor and oils. After the steeping time is up, strain the liquid to remove the rosemary leaves and discard the solids. Your rosemary tea is now ready to drink. To enjoy the benefits of rosemary for bad breath, drink the tea after meals or when you feel like you need a fresh breath. You can also add honey or lemon to the tea if you prefer a sweeter or tangier taste. It's essential to note that rosemary tea is not a replacement for regular oral hygiene practices, but it can help reduce bad breath caused by digestive issues or other factors.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus tea is a popular remedy for bad breath due to its antibacterial properties. It contains compounds like eucalyptol, which have been shown to kill bacteria in the mouth that can cause bad breath. When we drink eucalyptus tea, the eucalyptol is absorbed into our bloodstream and then released into our saliva. This helps to reduce the number of bacteria in our mouth, making it a more inhospitable place for the bacteria that cause bad breath to thrive. Additionally, eucalyptus globulus tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling in the gums and mouth, further contributing to a reduction in bad breath. By reducing the number of bacteria and inflammation in the mouth, eucalyptus globulus tea can help to leave our breath smelling fresher and cleaner.

Quercetin, commonly known as rosemary, is good for bad breath because it has antibacterial properties that help reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth, which is the main cause of bad breath.

remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal teas for bad breath
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Rosmarinus officinalis herbal tea that helps with bad breath, you'll need dried rosemary leaves. Start by boiling one cup of water in a kettle or on the stovetop. While the water is heating up, measure out one teaspoon of dried rosemary leaves and place it in a tea infuser or a small muslin bag. Once the water has reached a boil, reduce the heat to a simmer and carefully place the tea infuser or muslin bag into the water. Allow the rosemary leaves to steep for 5-7 minutes, or until the water has reduced slightly and the aroma of rosemary is noticeable. After the steeping time has passed, carefully remove the tea infuser or muslin bag from the water and discard the rosemary leaves. Strain the tea into a cup if necessary, then let it cool to a comfortable temperature before drinking. You can enjoy this rosemary tea hot or iced, and it's best to drink it in moderation, ideally 1-2 cups per day, to help reduce bad breath and promote overall oral health.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum tea, also known as Ceylon cinnamon tea, is a natural remedy for bad breath. This tea has antibacterial properties that help kill the bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath. The antibacterial compounds in Ceylon cinnamon tea, such as cinnamaldehyde, work by stopping the growth of these bacteria, which in turn reduces the production of volatile sulfur compounds that cause bad breath. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the mouth and gums, which can also contribute to bad breath. Drinking Ceylon cinnamon tea after meals can help prevent the bacteria in the mouth from multiplying and causing bad breath. The tea's antibacterial properties also help to kill the bacteria that can cause gum disease, another common cause of bad breath.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Rosmarinus officinalis herbal tea that can help with bad breath, start by gathering a handful of fresh rosemary leaves or one teaspoon of dried rosemary. Next, boil a cup of water and let it cool for a minute or two. Then, add the rosemary leaves to the hot water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes, depending on your preference for the strength of the tea. You can also add honey or lemon to taste, but be careful not to add too much sugar as it can counteract the benefits of the tea. After the steeping time, strain the tea into another cup and discard the rosemary leaves. Let the tea cool down for a minute before drinking it. Drinking this rosemary tea regularly can help to freshen your breath and reduce bad breath caused by gum disease, gum inflammation, or other mouth problems.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tea, also known as ginger tea, is a natural remedy for bad breath. The tea is made from the root of the ginger plant, which has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat various health issues. When you drink ginger tea, the active compounds in the ginger root, such as gingerol and shogaol, are released into your body. These compounds have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath. The anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, which can contribute to bad breath. Additionally, ginger tea has a spicy and warming effect, which can help to stimulate saliva production in the mouth. Saliva contains enzymes that help to break down food particles and bacteria, which can also help to reduce bad breath. By reducing the growth of bacteria, reducing inflammation, and stimulating saliva production, ginger tea can help to freshen your breath and prevent bad breath.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Rosmarinus officinalis herbal tea that helps with bad breath, start by gathering dried rosemary leaves, which can be found in most health food stores or online. Begin by boiling one liter of water in a large pot, then reduce the heat to a simmer. Next, add one teaspoon of dried rosemary leaves to the pot and let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the leaves to release their flavor and medicinal properties. Once the steeping time is up, remove the pot from the heat and let it sit for a few minutes before straining the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth. Discard the rosemary leaves and let the tea cool down to a comfortable drinking temperature. Finally, drink the tea as needed to help freshen your breath and alleviate bad breath. You can also add honey or lemon to taste, but be sure to drink it in moderation as excessive consumption may cause stomach upset.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris tea, also known as thyme tea, has been used for centuries to help with bad breath. Thyme is a plant that contains oils with antibacterial properties. When you drink thyme tea, these oils go into your mouth and help kill the bacteria that are causing bad breath. Bad breath is often caused by bacteria in the mouth that break down food, especially proteins, and release a strong smell. Thyme's antibacterial properties stop these bacteria from growing and multiplying, which helps reduce the smell of bad breath. Thyme tea also has antiseptic properties, which help reduce inflammation in the mouth and gums, making it harder for bacteria to grow. Drinking thyme tea can also help loosen and remove food particles from your teeth, which can get stuck and cause bad breath.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Rosmarinus officinalis herbal tea that helps with bad breath, start by gathering a handful of fresh or dried rosemary leaves. If using fresh leaves, gently rinse them with water and pat them dry to remove excess moisture. If using dried leaves, simply crush them into smaller pieces to release their flavor and oils. Next, place one teaspoon of the rosemary leaves into a teapot or a cup. Pour one cup of boiling water over the leaves and let it steep for five to seven minutes, depending on your desired strength of tea. After the steeping time has passed, strain the tea into a separate cup to remove the leaves. You can now drink the rosemary tea hot or cold, sweetened with honey if desired. Drinking one to two cups of rosemary tea a day may help reduce bad breath by its antibacterial properties and its ability to freshen breath.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus tea, also known as lemongrass tea, is a popular remedy for bad breath. This is because the tea contains compounds that have antibacterial properties, which help kill the bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath. These bacteria often thrive in areas with low oxygen levels, such as the back of the throat and tongue. The antibacterial properties in lemongrass tea help reduce the number of these bacteria, leading to fresher breath. Additionally, lemongrass tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe mouth irritations, such as sore gums or ulcers, which can also contribute to bad breath. Drinking lemongrass tea may also stimulate saliva production, which can help wash away bacteria and food particles that can cause bad breath.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Rosmarinus officinalis herbal tea that helps with bad breath, start by gathering fresh rosemary leaves. Choose a clean and dry place to dry the leaves, and tie them in small bunches. Hang the bunches upside down until they are completely dry, which may take a few days. Once the leaves are dry, you can store them in airtight containers. To make the tea, place one teaspoon of dried rosemary leaves in a cup, and pour one cup of boiling water over them. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea into another cup. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but be careful not to add too much sugar as rosemary is a strong herb. Drinking this tea regularly can help reduce bad breath by killing bacteria in the mouth and freshening your breath.

remedies.remedy_desc: Satureja montana tea is a natural remedy for bad breath. This tea is made from the leaves of the mountain savory plant, which contains compounds that help kill bacteria in the mouth. When bacteria in the mouth break down food, they release strong-smelling gases that cause bad breath. The active compounds in Satureja montana tea, such as carvacrol and thymol, are antibacterial agents that destroy these bacteria and prevent them from growing back. By reducing the number of bacteria in the mouth, Satureja montana tea helps to eliminate the source of bad breath. Additionally, this tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and calm the mouth, reducing redness and swelling that can contribute to bad breath. Drinking Satureja montana tea after meals can help to freshen breath and leave a pleasant, herbal flavor in the mouth.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Rosmarinus officinalis herbal tea that helps with bad breath, you will need fresh or dried rosemary leaves. Start by measuring out one tablespoon of the rosemary leaves for every cup of boiling water you plan to use. If you're using fresh rosemary, you can simply add the leaves to a teapot or infuser, while dried rosemary can be added directly to the cup. Next, bring the water to a boil and then pour it over the rosemary leaves. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, or until the liquid has cooled slightly. To help with bad breath, you can try adding a squeeze of lemon juice to the tea, as the acidity can help kill bacteria in the mouth. Once the tea has cooled, you can drink it as needed to help freshen your breath.

supplementary_best: A blend of 'Thymus vulgaris' (thyme), 'Cinnamomum verum' (cinnamon), and 'Zingiber officinale' (ginger) is a good combination to remedy bad breath. Thyme has antimicrobial properties that help kill the bacteria causing bad breath, while its expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the mouth and throat. Cinnamon has a natural antibacterial property that helps reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth. Ginger, with its anti-inflammatory properties, helps soothe the mouth and throat, reducing swelling and discomfort. When combined, these herbs work together to kill bacteria, reduce inflammation, and promote good oral health, making it an effective remedy for bad breath. This blend should be brewed as a tea by steeping 1 teaspoon of dried thyme, 1/2 teaspoon of cinnamon powder, and 1/2 teaspoon of dried ginger in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Strain and drink after it has cooled down.
supplementary_avoid: If you have bad breath, there are certain herbal teas you should avoid. One of these is tea made from the roots of licorice (Glycyrrhiza uralensis). Licorice root tea can cause an imbalance of bacteria in the mouth, which may worsen bad breath.  Another herb to avoid is dandelion (Taraxacum officinale), which can dry out your mouth. A dry mouth can lead to bad breath because it reduces saliva production, which normally helps wash away bacteria and food particles.  You should also avoid teas made from the roots of burdock (Arctium lappa) and sarsaparilla (Smilax ornata). Both of these herbs have been known to cause digestive issues and can lead to bad breath if not properly digested.  Additionally, avoid teas made from the roots of goldenseal (Hydrastis canadensis) and myrrh (Commiphora molmol) as they can cause digestive problems and irritation in the mouth.  Lastly, avoid teas made from the roots of valerian (Valeriana officinalis), as it can cause dry mouth and digestive issues, which can contribute to bad breath.
supplementary_other_remedies: Bad breath, also known as halitosis, can be caused by bacteria in the mouth. Besides drinking herbal teas, other natural remedies can help to reduce bad breath. One such remedy is oil pulling, which involves swishing oil in the mouth to kill bacteria. This is done by taking a tablespoon of oil, such as coconut or sesame oil, and swishing it in the mouth for several minutes before spitting it out. The bacteria in the mouth get stuck to the oil, making it easier to remove them. This can help to reduce bad breath by reducing the amount of bacteria in the mouth.  Another remedy is brushing and flossing regularly. This helps to remove food particles and plaque from the teeth, which can contribute to bad breath. When food particles are left in the mouth, they can rot and release foul-smelling gases. By removing these particles and plaque, you can reduce the amount of bacteria in the mouth and prevent bad breath. Additionally, brushing and flossing can also help to reduce gum inflammation, which can also cause bad breath.  Diet can also play a role in bad breath. Eating foods that are high in fiber, such as fruits and vegetables, can help to clean the teeth and mouth. These foods contain fiber that can help to scrub the teeth and remove bacteria. Additionally, eating foods that are high in water content, such as watermelon and celery, can help to keep the mouth moist and reduce the amount of bacteria in the mouth.  Finally, some people find that chewing gum can help to reduce bad breath. Chewing gum can stimulate saliva production in the mouth, which can help to wash away bacteria and food particles. This can help to reduce bad breath by removing the bacteria and food particles that contribute to it. However, not all gum is created equal, and some gum may contain sugars or other ingredients that can contribute to bad breath.  Mouthwashes can also be helpful. Some mouthwashes contain ingredients such as essential oils that can help to kill bacteria in the mouth. These ingredients can help to reduce bad breath by reducing the amount of bacteria in the mouth. However, some mouthwashes may contain harsh chemicals that can irritate the mouth and make bad breath worse.  Lastly, some people find that probiotics can help to reduce bad breath. Probiotics are live bacteria and yeasts that are beneficial for the body. They can help to reduce the amount of bad bacteria in the mouth, which can contribute to bad breath. Probiotics can be found in foods such as yogurt and kefir, or they can be taken as a supplement.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with bad breath often experience other health issues due to underlying problems like gum disease, sinus infections, or digestive problems. Medicinal herbs can help alleviate these issues. For instance, those with gum disease may also have bleeding gums, swollen gums, or loose teeth. Turmeric, with its anti-inflammatory properties, can help reduce swelling and fight infection. Its active compound, curcumin, can also prevent the growth of bacteria that cause gum disease.  Similarly, people with sinus infections may experience headaches, facial pain, or a runny nose. Echinacea, an herb with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, can help alleviate these symptoms. Its ability to boost the immune system can also help fight off the infection. Furthermore, those with digestive problems like indigestion, bloating, or stomach ulcers may have bad breath due to poor digestion or the presence of H. pylori bacteria. Licorice root, with its anti-inflammatory properties, can help soothe the stomach and reduce inflammation. Its ability to protect the stomach lining can also prevent ulcers.  Additionally, people with bad breath may also experience dry mouth, which can be caused by a lack of saliva or medication side effects. Aloe vera, with its soothing properties, can help moisturize the mouth and reduce inflammation. Its antibacterial properties can also help prevent the growth of bacteria that cause bad breath.
